title: Data Room NDA
description: A restrictive confidentiality agreement safeguarding access to the online virtual data room during M&A/funding rounds.
whatsCovered: Access control credentials and user log tracking
Prohibition on copying, printing, or exporting data files
Mandatory destruction of data room files upon demand
whenToUse: Use this NDA before providing prospective investors or corporate buyers with access to your virtual data room (VDR) containing your cap table, audits, and source code.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: DATA ROOM NDA
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Data Room NDA. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Virtual Data Room Restrictions
clauses.body: Investor {receiving_investor} is granted access to data room at {data_room_url}. Copying, downloading, or sharing files is strictly prohibited.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Why do we need a data room NDA?
faq.answer: It places strict rules on the digital data space, prohibiting downloading, copying, or forwarding the proprietary documents inside.

faq.question: Does this agreement track user logs?
faq.answer: Yes, it contractually permits the company to monitor IP logs and document views within the VDR platform.
